description_full:
Wir gehören zu den Top-Adressen in der IT-Welt und fokussieren uns auf die Optimierung der Kerngeschäftsprozesse unserer Kunden. Durch den smarten Einsatz maßgeschneiderter IT-Lösungen unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, das Beste aus ihrem Business und aktuellen Technologien herauszuholen. Unseren Erfolg aber erreichen wir nur durch eins: die Menschen bei adesso! Sie bringen Ideen, fachliches Know how und Technologien zusammen. Gemeinsam programmieren wir (deine) Zukunft bei adesso.

DEINE ROLLE


Analytics-Konzeption: Entwickle Analytics-Strategien und konzipiere individuelle Lösungen für komplexe Herausforderungen in Web- und App-Projekten.
Implementierung: Implementiere Analytics-Tools und richte Tag-Management-, Tracking- und Consent-Management-Systeme für unsere Kunden ein.
Reporting & Dashboards: Erstelle Berichte und interaktive Dashboards, visualisiere Ergebnisse und interpretiere diese gemeinsam mit internen Teams im Online Marketing.
Compliance & Prozesse: Optimiere Prozesse und unterstütze unsere Kunden dabei, technische und rechtliche Anforderungen wie die Cookie-Compliance einzuhalten.
Wissensvermittlung: Vermittle Wissen, indem du interne und externe Schulungen zu Analytics-Themen durchführst.
Strategische Beratung: Berate Kunden und begleite sie beim strategischen Ausbau ihres Trackings.


DEIN PROFIL


Analytics- und Tracking-Systeme: Durch deine mehrjährige Berufserfahrung (mind. 3 Jahre) kennst du bereits Tools wie Piwik PRO, Matomo oder Google Analytics und bist mit Tag-Management- und Dashboard-Systemen vertraut. Aber auch Consent Management Plattformen sind dir nicht unbekannt und du verstehst die Funktion von Cookie Bannern auf Webseiten und in Mobile Apps.
Online-Marketing Expertise: Insbesondere datengetriebene Kampagnen gehören zu deinem Fachwissen.
Technisches Verständnis: Du bringst fundiertes Know-how in Webtechnologien und Programmiersprachen wie HTML, CSS und JavaScript mit.
Kenntnisse zur strategischen Analytics-Beratung: Idealerweise bringst du erste Erfahrung mit ganzheitlichen Datenstrategien für Unternehmen mit und bist begeisterungsfähig für technologische Entwicklungen und Trends im Analytics-Bereich.
Kommunikationsstärke: Du bist vertriebsorientiert und begeisterst andere für das Thema Digital Marketing.
KI Kompetenz: Du setzt KI-Tools in deinem Fachgebiet souverän ein, bewertest die Ergebnisse kritisch und unterstützt Kunden und adessi dabei, Potenziale von KI zu nutzen.
Sprachkenntnisse: Deine sehr guten Deutsch- und guten Englischkenntnisse ermöglichen dir eine sichere Kommunikation.


Unser Versprechen: Du wirst dich bei uns wohlfühlen! Kollegial, familiär und auf Augenhöhe – wir leben Austausch, Teamgeist und einen respektvollen Umgang miteinander. Diversität und verschiedene Blickwinkel werden bei uns genauso geschätzt wie du als Mensch. Das und viel mehr steht für unser ganz besonderes Wir-Gefühl. Für das es sogar ein Wort gibt: adessi.

Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. So belegen wir zum wiederholten Mal den 1. Platz beim Wettbewerb „Deutschlands Beste Arbeitgeber in der ITK“!. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. Denn gemeinsam zu wachsen und Chancengeber zu sein, das ist programmiert in unserer DNA.

description_full:
(This is a Germany-based remote role. Fluent English is required; German is strongly preferred for localization/partner work, but not mandatory.)







Your Future Employer:

This purpose-driven analytics platform is trusted by 150,000+ organisations — from Porsche, LEGO, and NASA to agile marketing agencies. Known for its strong stance on privacy, GDPR compliance, and data ownership, it offers both SaaS cloud and on-premise deployment.




Germany is one of the company’s most mature markets (c. €5–6M ARR), historically driven by product-led growth. As the business expands its sales-led motion (outbound), Germany remains a priority market alongside France and the US, with increasing focus on mid-market and enterprise segments.










Your Role as Product Marketing Manager:

This is a hands-on, execution-focused Product Marketing role. You will connect Product, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success by translating product capabilities into clear, differentiated, and commercially relevant messaging.




You will be expected to define positioning and messaging — and also produce the core assets yourself (not just coordinate others). Key responsibilities include:




Product understanding & communication

Build a strong understanding of Matomo’s products, use cases, and roadmap
Translate product features into clear, compelling messaging across marketing, sales, and partner channels
Ensure consistency and clarity of product communication across all touchpoints

Positioning, USP & competitive differentiation

Define and maintain product positioning and messaging frameworks
Articulate clear USPs for different ICPs and use cases
Build and maintain competitive intelligence (analysis, comparisons, battlecards, objection handling)
Ensure differentiation is consistently reflected in customer-facing materials

ICP understanding & sales enablement

Develop a strong understanding of ICPs, segments, and buyer needs
Create practical, high-quality enablement materials, including: battlecards, one-pagers, pitch decks, use case narratives, objection-handling resources
Support go-to-market readiness for launches and campaigns

Translating product into business value

Frame messaging around customer outcomes and commercial value (“why it matters”)
Adapt narratives for technical and non-technical audiences
Enable Sales, Customer Success, and partners to communicate value effectively

Cross-functional collaboration

Work closely with Product, Sales, Customer Success, and Marketing teams
Gather feedback from customer-facing teams and continuously refine messaging
Help improve alignment between product development and go-to-market execution







Who You Are

Experience: 4–6+ years in Product Marketing, Go-to-Market, or Sales Enablement in B2B SaaS

Proven track record of personally creating: messaging frameworks, positioning documents, battlecards, sales decks, one-pagers, competitor comparisons
Experience defining positioning, USPs, and differentiation
Strong experience in competitive analysis and enablement
Comfortable working with technical or complex products

Way of working

Hands-on and execution-oriented (not a coordination-only profile)
Able to operate as an individual contributor who both defines and delivers
Strong communicator, able to translate product into business value
Structured, clear thinker with strong attention to messaging quality
Collaborative and effective in cross-functional, remote environments

Nice to have

Experience in analytics, martech, privacy, or data-related SaaS
Exposure to PLG → SLG environments
Experience with partner enablement
Experience in technically complex or competitive markets

Languages & Location

Fluent English required, German is a strong plus
Remote within Germany







Why Should Someone Go for This Opportunity?

💰 €75,000–€85,000 base salary.

🌐 Remote-first within Germany.

🌍 Mission with impact: ethical, privacy-first analytics used by millions.

🚀 High strategic influence as the first PMM

Dabei hilft uns ein hochkompetentes Team, das die gesamte Klaviatur des digitalen Marketings beherrscht und mit Hilfe des interdisziplinären Know-hows kontinuierlich neue Ansätze entwickelt um unsere Kunden am Markt erfolgreich zu positionieren.




Deine Aufgaben




Mit einem Mix aus bewährten Consulting Methoden und Werkzeugen sowie einem Schuss Start-Up Mentalität kreieren wir gemeinsam Kundenerlebnisse, die den Menschen den größtmöglichen Mehrwert bringen. Als Consultant Digital Analytics (m/w/d) in Hamburg übernimmst Du bei uns die zentrale Verantwortung für:

Konzeption und Implementierung von Tracking (z. B. Google Tag Manager) und Web Analytics (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, ggf. weitere)
Konzeption und Implementierung von Berichten und Dashboards zur Analyse und Unterstützung von Geschäftsprozessen und –feldern sowie Nutzerverhalten

Dein Profil

Wir suchen Menschen, keine Maschinen. Deswegen solltest du vor allem Spaß an deiner Arbeit haben. Ganz ohne Hard Skills geht es natürlich nicht. Um alle Herausforderungen zu meistern, wünschen wir uns von dir:

ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium
Erfahrung im Agentur-, Inhouse- und/oder Medienbereich mit Analytics-Fokus
Fundierte Kenntnisse in den gängigen Tracking- und Analyse-Tools (z.B. Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Adobe Analytics, Adobe Launch, etc.)
eine ausgeprägte analytische Denkweise und die Fähigkeit, mit Daten umzugehen (SQL, R oder Python)
Gutes Verständnis von Digital Marketing und Attribution
Schnell Auffassungsgabe und hohe Lernbereitschaft

Damit sammelst du Pluspunkte:

Erfahrung im Storytelling via Datenvisualisierung in den gängigen Tools und Medien (Google Data Studio, PowerBI, Keynote, Powerpoint)
Kenntnisse in Web-Technologien und -Sprachen (z.B. HTML, CSS, Javascript, etc.)




Dein Extra

• Flexible Arbeitsgestaltung und Vertrauensarbeitszeitt

• Neueste Technologien und Know-how

• 30 Tage Urlaub + frei an deinem Geburtstag

description_full:
If you want to be a Maxonian and help bring the best 2D and 3D visual effects, motion graphics, gaming, AR/MR/VR, visualization and design software to the market, then we should have a chat.




WHO WE ARE:

Packed with brilliant, passionate people, Maxon is deeply rooted in the creative industry and committed to empowering the artistic community. We are Maxonians. We encourage and motivate each other to be curious learners. We are obsessed with customer fulfillment and inspiration – before, during and after purchase. If you too want to be a Maxonian and help bring to market the finest software products for 2D and 3D visual effects, motion graphics, games, AR/MR/VR, visualization and general design, let’s talk.




What you will do:




As a Marketing Analytics Lead, you are accountable for how marketing tracking, attribution, and measurement are designed, implemented, and trusted at Maxon.




This role sits at the intersection of Marketing, Finance, Engineering, and Analytics. You set direction and standards for marketing measurement so performance, ROI, and experimentation can be evaluated consistently and with confidence across channels.




This role goes beyond analysis and reporting. You own the measurement foundation and ensure it is correctly implemented and maintained as marketing activities evolve.




Marketing tracking and instrumentation




Own end to end marketing tracking across web and marketing channels
Define tracking standards, event taxonomies, and conversion definitions
Ensure tracking remains correct as campaigns, funnels, and products change
Provide clear requirements and oversight for tracking implementations
Act as the final point of accountability for tracking coverage and correctness




Funnels, measurement, and attribution




Define how marketing performance is measured from impression to conversion
Own attribution approaches, assumptions, and limitations
Lead measurement for incrementality testing and experimentation
Ensure consistent calculation of CAC, ROAS, revenue, and marketing ROI across channels




Data quality and validation




Own data quality across marketing measurement systems
Establish validation processes and monitoring to detect issues early
Drive resolution of discrepancies across platforms and downstream systems
Communicate data limitations, confidence levels, and caveats clearly




Analytics Tools & Systems




Act as system owner for GA4, including event schema, conversion definitions, and configuration
Own Google Tag Manager (GTM) setup, structure, and governance
Implement and validate tagging across web properties and marketing platforms
Ensure accurate data flow from GA4 into downstream reporting tools (e.g. Looker / Looker Studio)
Troubleshoot tracking discrepancies and data quality issues across tools




Leadership and collaboration




Act as the primary owner and decision maker for marketing tracking and attribution
Lead alignment across Marketing, Finance, Engineering, Product, and Analytics
Translate business questions into clear measurement approaches
Enable teams to run reliable experiments and make informed optimization decisions




What we are looking for:




Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
3–6+ years of experience in marketing, web, or app analytics
Hands‑on experience owning GA4 implementations, event tagging, and GTM
Experience building marketing dashboards and models in Looker / Looker Studio or similar tools
Strong understanding of attribution, funnel analysis, and data quality validation




BENEFITS:




We want you to feel comfortable with us - not just during working hours, but every second. Maxon provides a range of benefits, here’s an overview of what you can expect.




Canteen with lunch subsidy
Job ticket
Snacks in the office
Modern equipment, laptops for employees
Nemetschek Corporate Benefits Program
Training budget
Free drinks (coffee, tea, water)
Connection to public transport

description_full:
||Remark: The following position requires fluent German language skills (min. level C1), both written and spoken.




Deine Aufgaben

Zur Verstärkung unserer Data & Analytics Platform Community suchen wir Kolleg:innen, um in diesem Bereich moderne Datenplattformen und KI-Lösungen für nationale und internationale Kunden zu entwickeln und zu betreiben.

In der Rolle des Architekten w/m/d - Data & Analytics Platform führst Du Deine Plattform-Teams und setzt die Plattformen nach agilen und modernen Methoden als Produkt bei den Kunden auf und etablierst diese.




Du bist verantwortlich für das Design und die Implementierung einer Data- und Analytics-Lösung.
Du arbeitest eng mit den Stakeholdern zusammen, um deren Bedürfnisse zu verstehen und technische Lösungen zu entwickeln.
Du führst Teams fachlich an, die Deine Architekturentwürfe umsetzen und unterstützt bei der Teamzusammenstellung.
Du akquirierst neue Projekte und überzeugst in Workshops durch Deine fachliche Expertise.




Dein Profil

Langjährige Berufserfahrung in den Bereichen IT-Architektur, Presales und Beratung, insbesondere im Lösungsdesign von Data- und Analytics-Architekturen
Umfangreiche Kenntnisse gängiger Data-Analytics-Plattformen, insbesondere Tools zur Verarbeitung der Daten, Analyse und Visualisierung
Kenntnisse in verschiedenen Bereichen wie Cloud-Lösungen, agile Methoden, CI/CD-Praktiken, Containerisierung und Cloud-Sicherheit werden vorausgesetzt
Projekterfahrung mit gehobenen Mittelstands- und Großkunden sowie ein souveränes Auftreten in Kundenbeziehungen und auf Managementebene
Hohe Eigenmotivation, Präsentationsstärke, kontinuierliche Weiterbildung und verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse




Wir bieten

Mobile Working & flexible Arbeitszeiten – Vertrauen ist gut. Punkt.
Urlaub – Auszeit gefällig? Mindestens 30 Tage Urlaub plus Brauchtumstage und bezahlten Sonderurlaub.
Mobilität – Günstiger unterwegs mit unserem Firmenwagen-Programm. Selbstverständlich auch mit dem E-Auto.
Diversity & Inclusion – Es gibt viel zu tun. Wir packen es bereits an, u. a. über Future-Leadership-Programme für Frauen, länderspezifische „People Panels“ oder interne Gruppen der LGBT+-Community.
Miteinander – Wir sind stolz auf unsere Consulting-Community aus echten Profis. Auch in Sachen Hilfsbereitschaft und Zusammenhalt! Über Länder und Teams hinweg.
Vergünstigungen – Profi-Equipment auch privat nutzen: neueste Hardware zu steuervergünstigten Leasingkonditionen mit dem Mitarbeiter:innen-PC-Programm.




Über uns

Computacenter ist ein internationales IT-Unternehmen mit einer 40-jährigen Erfolgsgeschichte. Allein in Deutschland bringen rund 7.000 Menschen die digitale Transformation voran: Sie entwickeln IT-Strategien, implementieren neue Technologien oder managen komplexe Infrastrukturen – für die Mehrheit der DAX-Unternehmen ebenso wie für große öffentliche Auftraggeber.




Als unabhängiger Partner aller führenden IT-Hersteller (wie z. B. Microsoft, Apple, IBM, HP, Cisco, Intel) sind wir in allen relevanten Technologiebereichen unterwegs: von Cyber Security, Cloud und Künstlicher Intelligenz über agile Software-Entwicklung bis hin zu Arbeitsplatzlösungen für die Smart Factory.




Und das Beste an uns? Ganz einfach: Die Menschen, die bei uns arbeiten, in ihrer Vielfalt, mit ihrem Potenzial und ihren individuellen Lebensentwürfen.




Klingt gut? Ist auch gut! Jetzt bewerben.




Deine Online-Bewerbung

Wir freuen uns auf Deine Online-Bewerbung, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft sowie Religion. Bewerbungen von schwerbehinderten Menschen sind bei Computacenter willkommen und werden bei gleicher Eignung bevorzugt berücksichtigt.

description_full:
About the Company




Who are we? TASKING is a leading provider of development tools headquartered in Munich, Germany, offering high-performance, high-quality, safety & security-oriented embedded software development tools for multi-core architectures. Our development tools are used by automotive manufacturers and suppliers, as well as in adjacent markets around the world to realize high-performance applications in safety-critical areas. As the recognized leader in high-quality, feature- and safety-compliant embedded software development tools, TASKING enables you to create code with best-in-class size and performance with compilers, debuggers and RTOS support for industry-leading microprocessors and microcontrollers.

Our customers are served from sales offices in the EU, US, UK, China, Japan, Korea and India. Product development facilities are located in Munich (DE), Amersfoort (NL), Liverpool (UK) and Ljubljana (SI).







About the Role




Are you interested in analyzing how marketing data influences meaningful business insights? Do you enjoy connecting numbers with real commercial impact? We’re looking for a Marketing Analytics Working Student to help us measure, understand, and optimize how marketing drives revenue in a growing international tech company.







Responsibilities




Build and maintain reports that track marketing-influenced pipeline and revenue
Analyse Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) and lead-to-revenue journey performance
Support multi-touch attribution modelling
Analyse campaign performance across digital and offline channels
Identify trends, patterns and optimisation opportunities
Support leadership reporting through dashboards and insights
Work with CRM and marketing automation data to ensure accuracy and consistency
Help improve tracking, attribution logic and reporting processes







Qualifications




Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s programme in Business Analytics, Marketing, Data Science, Business Administration, Economics or similar







Required Skills




Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Familiarity with Excel with a willingness to quickly learn additional software tools such as PowerBI (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis), marketing automation platforms, and CRM systems (such as Salesforce)
Basic understanding of marketing funnels and B2B revenue models
Structured, reliable and comfortable working with data sets
Fluent in English (German is a plus but not required)
Confident communicator with great interpersonal skills







What You`ll Gain




Real-world experience connecting marketing activity to revenue outcomes
Exposure to B2B SaaS growth metrics and commercial strategy
Hands-on experience with attribution modelling and pipeline analysis
Close collaboration with senior marketing and revenue leaders
Flexible working hours to fit your studies in Munich







Pay range and compensation package




This role goes beyond reporting. You’ll help shape how our company understands marketing performance and makes data-driven decisions. Furthermore, you will work in an informal, international, professionally challenging workspace, with a hybrid working environment to fit with your personal needs. And, it goes without saying that you will be compensated competitively, according to an international benchmark.







Equal Opportunity Statement




If you’re excited about analytics, curious about revenue mechanics, and want to build a strong foundation in marketing performance measurement, then we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and help us turn data into growth.




TASKING complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and any other data protection laws or regulations which apply to TASKING. The company’s data privacy policy is also available on the company’s website: https://www.tasking.com/privacy-policy

Du steuerst Budgets eigenverantwortlich und sorgst dafür, dass erfolgreiche Kampagnen skalierbar werden.
Du dokumentierst deine Tests, Entscheidungen und Erkenntnisse sauber, damit Performance-Marketing bei Freeway systematisch besser wird.




Wichtige Skills & Kompetenzen:




Fachlich & analytisch

Sehr gute Erfahrung im Performance Marketing, insbesondere auf Meta und LinkedIn
Starkes Verständnis für B2B Leadgenerierung
Erfahrung mit Funnel-Strategie und Conversion-optimierten Kampagnen
Sicherheit in Landingpage-Optimierung, Creative Testing und Conversion Tracking
Fähigkeit, Daten sauber zu analysieren und daraus konkrete Maßnahmen abzuleiten
Verständnis für die Zusammenhänge von CPL, Cost per Show, Cost per Call und Cost per Customer

 

Idealerweise bringst du Erfahrung mit:

Webinar-Funnels als zentralem Growth-Hebel
Info-Produkten, Coaching oder beratungsnahen Geschäftsmodellen
größeren Werbebudgets und skalierenden Kampagnenstrukturen

Du denkst analytisch und gleichzeitig unternehmerisch.
Du arbeitest systematisch statt hektisch.
Du hast Ownership für Ergebnisse, nicht nur für Maßnahmen.
Du dokumentierst Learnings sauber und baust daraus ein echtes Testing-System.
Du willst nicht einfach Ads schalten, sondern ein funktionierendes Wachstumsmodell bauen.
Du verstehst Funnel als System – nicht als Aneinanderreihung einzelner Maßnahmen.




Was wir dir bieten:

Yamaha is the world’s biggest manufacturer of musical instruments and one of the leading producers of audio equipment for home entertainment and professional use. Our European headquarters are located in Rellingen (northwest of Hamburg). We have approximately 800 dedicated employees working in our offices across Europe including Germany, France, Italy, Spain, Scandinavia, and the UK, who operate local and international sales and marketing activities.

To Strengthen The Digital Marketing Capabilities Across The Music Instruments Business, With a Strong Focus On Performance And Long-term Customer Value, We Are Looking For a Temporary Colleague (m/f/d) For The Duration Of Parental Leave

Paid Advertising Specialist (m/f/d) - limited until July 31st, 2027 -

In this role you will be responsible for planning, executing, monitoring, and optimizing paid advertising activities in a cross-divisional role. This position works closely with internal stakeholders, external agencies, and platform partners to ensure effective media planning, campaign execution, and performance reporting across the European region.

Your Duties


Set up, activate, monitor, and optimize paid advertising campaigns across platforms including Google, Meta, YouTube, and TikTok in a cross-divisional environment
Serve as a key contact point for external platform partners
Plan campaign objectives, budgets, timelines, channel allocation, and account structures
Analyze performance data on a daily basis to ensure spend efficiency and conduct deeper performance reviews to identify trends and optimization opportunities
Coordinate creative assets, messaging, keywords, and local market adaptations, ensuring alignment with brand voice and campaign objectives
Develop audience strategies, including retargeting and performance signals
Monitor campaign performance, conduct A/B testing, and implement ongoing optimizations to improve results and customer experience
Communicate campaign performance, insights, successes, and challenges clearly to stakeholders, including recommendations for future strategies
Prepare and present regular performance reports and post-campaign evaluations
Track campaign spend and manage monthly invoicing processes
Monitor market trends, platform developments, and competitor activity, applying relevant insights to campaign planning


Your Profile


Proven experience in paid advertising or performance marketing across major digital platforms
Strong understanding of campaign optimization, performance analysis, and media planning
Experience working with agencies and cross-functional internal teams
Analytical approach with knowledge of translating data into actionable insights
Structured, reliable, and detail-oriented working style
Good communication skills, with fluency in English


We Offer


An internationally active company that is characterized by sustainable action for the environment, customers and employees
A competitive, attractive product range
An international, committed team with a strong team spirit
Work-life balance through a 38.5-hour week, our flextime model and the option of mobile working
30 days of paid leave per year
Fair remuneration based on collective agreements including Christmas and holiday bonuses plus performance-related bonuses
Possibility of a company pension scheme
Subsidy for public transport
Individual training opportunities

If you are interested in this position, please submit your application including your earliest possible start date and salary expectations via our online application form.

description_full:
It starts with nature. With you. Helfen Sie uns, im Rahmen einer Elternzeitvertretung bis voraussichtlich Januar 2028, unsere Marketingprozesse kanalübergreifend zu steuern als:

Omnichannel Solutions Manager (w/m/d) - HubSpot Marketing Automation & DAM

Ort: Karlsruhe, hybrid

Firma: Dr. Willmar Schwabe

Bereich: Commercial

Start: 01.05.2026

Arbeitszeitmodell: Vollzeit

Anstellungsart: befristetes Arbeitsverhältnis

Ihre Wachstum beflügelnden Aufgaben


HubSpot-Setup: Sie bauen unsere Marketing-Plattform für Deutschland hands-on auf, testen E-Mail-Strecken, Landingpages, Gewinnspiele und Social-Connects, bis jede Journey begeistert.
DAM-Governance: Sie kuratieren unser Digital Asset Management, definieren Upload-Profile, beseitigen Duplikate und stellen sicher, dass jedes Asset punktgenau verfügbar ist.
Prozess-Architektur: Sie übersetzen Marketingideen in skalierbare Workflows, harmonisieren Lifecycle-Stages und integrieren das Event-Management-Modul.
Schnittstellen-Orchestrierung: Sie verbinden CRM, HubSpot und Event-Tools, steuern externe Dienstleister und halten alle Stakeholder auf Erfolgskurs.
Change-Management: Sie schulen Nutzer, fördern Akzeptanz und begleiten unseren Kulturwandel hin zu echten Omnichannel-Erlebnissen.


Ihr natürlich überzeugendes Profil


Fachliche Anforderungen: Sie verfügen über ein abgeschlossenes wirtschaftswissenschaftliches Studium sowie fundierte Praxis in Marketing-Automation und Digitalprojekten.
Erfahrung: Sie konnten bereits mehrjährige Hands-on-Expertise mit HubSpot oder einem anderen digitalen Marketing-Tool sammeln, idealerweise im Pharma-Umfeld. Change-Management und Nutzeraktivierung/-kommunikation sind dabei ebenfalls keine Fremdworte für Sie.
Kompetenzen: Ihr starkes Prozess- und Technologieverständnis wird durch strukturierte Projektsteuerung und Priorisierung sowie ausgeprägtes Stakeholder-Management ergänzt.
Persönliche Stärken: Persönlich überzeugen Sie uns durch ein souveränes, kommunikatives und proaktives Auftreten.
Sprachkenntnisse: Ihre fließenden Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse runden Ihr Profil ab.


Ihre von Natur aus wertschätzenden Vorteile


Tarifvertrag Chemie: tarifvertragliche Vergütung inkl. Weihnachts- und Urlaubsgeld sowie attraktive Zusatzleistungen wie Pflegezusatz-, Berufsunfähigkeitsversicherung und arbeitgeberfinanzierte Altersvorsorge
Work-Life-Balance: 37,5 Stunden Arbeitswoche, 30 Tage Urlaub plus flexibel fünf zusätzliche Urlaubstage oder Sonderzahlung, Gleitzeit und die Möglichkeit zu flexiblen Arbeitszeiten
Gesundheit und Wohlbefinden: betriebsärztlicher Dienst, Betriebssportangebote (z.B. Yoga) und täglich frische Gerichte in unserer Kantine
Weiterbildung: Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten durch die Schwabe Academy sowie externe Anbieter
Sonstiges: Langzeitkonto z.B. zur Nutzung für ein Sabbatical, kostenlose Parkmöglichkeiten, Rabatte für Fitnessstudios, freizeitliche Aktivitäten und Shopping, familiäre Arbeitsatmosphäre mit der Sicherheit eines wachsenden Familienunternehmens


Über Dr. Willmar Schwabe

From Nature. For Health. Dr. Willmar Schwabe ist der weltweit führende Hersteller pflanzlicher Arzneimittel. Als Familienunternehmen mit über 150-jähriger Geschichte und weltweit rund 4.000 Mitarbeitenden stehen wir für Produkte von außergewöhnlich hoher Qualität. Um die Gesundheit von Menschen auf der ganzen Welt zu fördern, setzen wir auf moderne Forschung, Kundenorientierung sowie kompetente und motivierte Mitarbeitende.

Persönlicher Kontakt

Nora Feskens ist telefonisch erreichbar unter +49 721 4005-342.

Dr. Willmar Schwabe GmbH & Co. KG

Willmar-Schwabe-Str. 4

76227 Karlsruhe

Bildung kann die Welt zu einem besseren Ort machen. Davon sind wir vom Friedrich Verlag fest überzeugt. Junior-Data-Analytics- und Marketing-Automation-Manager m/w/d

Deine Mission:

Du kommst frisch von der Uni oder stehst am Anfang deiner Karriere und hast ein Händchen für Zahlen, Tools und clevere Marketingstrategien?

Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig!

Wir suchen einen Data-Analytics- & Marketing-Automation-Spezialisten (m/w/d), der datengetriebenes Marketing auf das nächste Level hebt - mit fundierten Analysen, smarten Segmentierungen und automatisierten Customer Journeys.

Das sind deine Aufgaben, die du nach gründlicher Einarbeitung übernimmst:


Du analysierst Website- und Marketing-Daten, bewertest Performance-Kennzahlen von Websites und leitest fundierte Insights aus Kampagnendaten ab.
Du sorgst für eine valide und konsistente Datenerfassung über verschiedene Analytics-Tools und Marketing-Kanäle hinweg - inklusive Unterstützung bei Tracking-Implementierung und -Monitoring.
Du prüfst kontinuierlich die Datenqualität, identifizierst Tracking-Fehler, Probleme in Data-Pipelines oder Dashboards und wirkst aktiv an der technischen Optimierung mit.
Du entwickelst Reporting- und Analyse-Dashboards, verknüpfst unterschiedliche Datenquellen und stellst datenbasierte Entscheidungsgrundlagen bereit.
Du analysierst die Performance von Website-Kampagnen, bewertest Traffic und Nutzerverhalten und leitest konkrete, umsetzbare Handlungsempfehlungen ab.
Du konzipierst und realisierst automatisierte, conversionstarke Kampagnen entlang des gesamten Lead- und Customer-Lifecycles - von Welcome- und Onboarding-Flows über Reaktivierung bis hin zu Upselling-Maßnahmen.
Du führst Zielgruppensegmentierungen durch und nutzt sie zur gezielten Kampagnensteuerung.


Das bringst du mit:


Ein abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Marketing, Kommunikation, digitale Medien, BWL oder einem vergleichbaren Fach - egal ob Bachelor oder Master.
Neugier und Interesse daran, das Verhalten von Nutzer:innen auf Websites besser zu verstehen - z. B. mithilfe von Zahlen, Auswertungen oder digitalen Tools. 
Begeisterung für neue Technologien und Trends im digitalen Marketing - insbesondere dafür, wie künstliche Intelligenz Marketingprozesse smarter und effizienter machen kann.
Spaß an digitalen Tools, Zahlen und Zusammenhängen sowie Lust, dich in Themen wie Datenanalyse und automatisiertes Marketing weiter einzuarbeiten.
Eine klare und offene Kommunikation, ein gutes Gespür für Zielgruppen und Freude daran, kreative Ideen für Kampagnen zu entwickeln.
Zuverlässigkeit, Teamgeist und Empathie - kombiniert mit der Motivation, Neues zu lernen, Dinge auszuprobieren und gemeinsam zu wachsen.


Das ist für dich drin:


Eine faire Vergütung und 32 Tage Urlaub
Ein richtig gutes Onboarding plus interne & externe Weiterbildungen, damit du schnell durchstartest
New-Work-Konzept mit größtmöglicher Flexibilität - keine Kernarbeitszeiten, großzügige Mobile-Office-Regelung
Gelegentliche Get-togethers im Verlag; Präsenzarbeit im Verlag ist immer möglich
Helles und gut erreichbares Büro mit moderner technischer Ausstattung im Herzen von Hannover, das Kreativität und Teamwork fördert

description_full:
About Wolt

At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.

Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.

Are you excited about building high-performing operations? Are you interested in understanding how to help couriers to perform at their best and maximize their earning potential while meeting Wolt's growth and profitability targets? Are you interested in becoming an expert in food delivery efficiency and continuously improving and adjusting our delivery operations set-up?

If this describes you, then we would be delighted to connect with you and explore this exciting opportunity further. Let's have a chat!

Role background

We launched our service in Germany in August 2020 and have, for the past years, seen rapid development in the market. We are now working with several thousand partners across all Germany and expanding further into new cities, regions, and business verticals.

To support our continued growth in Germany, we are looking for an Operations Analyst – Pricing & Efficiency to join our Germany Operations team. In this role, you will support the execution of pricing and efficiency initiatives that improve the effectiveness of our delivery operations.

This is a full-time position, reporting directly to the Pricing & Efficiency Manager within Germany Operations. You will work closely with senior team members who will set direction and priorities, while you focus on hands-on analysis, execution, and operational follow-through.

Your work will contribute to city-level pricing updates, operational efficiency improvements, and courier supply–demand balance in the German market. The role sits at the intersection of data analysis and business execution, offering strong exposure to how operational decisions are made in a high-growth marketplace.

What You'll Be Doing

You will focus on execution and analysis, with clear guidance and direction from senior leadership. Key responsibilities include:


Supporting the implementation of city-level courier pricing changes under the German operations model.
Producing analyses and insights to support pricing and efficiency initiatives led by senior team members.
Executing recurring and ad hoc analyses to monitor performance, identify inefficiencies, and track impact.
Supporting operational experiments (e.g. pricing or efficiency tests), including data preparation, analysis, and documentation of results.
Helping adapt and roll out global pricing and efficiency initiatives in the German market.
Collaborating closely with local Operations teams, Finance, Product, and Global Pricing & Efficiency stakeholders.
Maintains a strong interest in and comprehensive knowledge of all market dynamics, ensuring that responsibilities align with and adapt to business changes.


Our humble expectations


2+ years of experience in a relevant field (operations, analytics, consulting, strategy, or similar).
Strong spreadsheet and SQL skills, with experience working on large datasets.
Nice to have: Experience with Looker or similar BI tools. Exposure to A/B testing or experimentation.
Ability to translate data into clear insights that support business decisions.
Hands-on mindset with interest in operational execution and continuous improvement.
Passion for efficiency and process optimization - you thrive on making things work better, faster, and more effectively.

Company Description

We are REPA Deutschland GmbH, part of the REPA Group - Europe's leading distributor of spare parts for the catering industry, commercial kitchens, refrigeration technology and more. With over 95,000 items from more than 2,000 manufacturers, we offer the largest range on the market. Our 6 warehouses in Europe, 9 locations and over 1,500 employees guarantee fast and reliable service. Our affiliation with Parts Town, the American market leader in high-tech spare parts distribution, gives us even more power. Together we ensure that everything runs smoothly - whether it's a coffee machine, refrigerator or combi-steamer.

Work location: Bergkirchen | From the end of 2027: Unterschleißheim

At REPA, we ensure that spare parts for commercial kitchens and professional appliances are where they need to be—fast. As a (Junior) Pricing Analyst (m/f/d), you’ll shape our international pricing strategies and help deliver the best value to our customers. If you love working with data, enjoy cross-functional teamwork, and thrive in a structured yet agile environment, this is your opportunity to make an impact.

Job Description


Continuously improve REPA’s international pricing strategies in close collaboration with sales, product management, and procurement
Support daily pricing operations, including implementing and maintaining price lists and handling pricing requests
Conduct price and margin simulations to support business decisions
Monitor and analyze key pricing KPIs to identify trends and optimization opportunities
Assist in project work related to pricing initiatives and process improvements


Qualifications


Completed studies in business administration, business informatics, or a related field
Strong analytical skills and a structured, result-oriented mindset
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Fluent in English (C1) and conversional in German (B2), both written and spoken
Experience in pricing considered a huge plus
Nice to have: Experience with a pricing software (e.g. PFX, PROS, ...)
High attention to detail and problem-solving abilities
Collaborative spirit with a desire to work in an international, cross-functional team
Eagerness to grow and take initiative in a dynamic work environment


Additional Information

In addition to a secure job with a highly varied, broad range of tasks in an internationally successful, fast-growing and family-friendly group of companies, you can also expect depending on location:


Attractive compensation package including Christmas and holiday bonuses
Flexible working hours and a flextime account for better work-life balance
Company pension scheme
Canteen with subsidized lunch
A positive, inclusive, and appreciative work atmosphere with flat hierarchies
Tailored learning and development opportunities to support your growth
Regular employee events
Employees recruit employees - bonus
Corporate Benefits
Free employee parking


REPA embraces diversity and is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability.

description_full:
We are seeking an experienced Analyst, Pricing & Revenue Optimization, to join our Data Science & Analytics team as a strategic partner and technical expert!

In this role, you will steer our pricing strategy and commercial analytics function, translating complex data into actionable business insights that directly impact revenue and profitability. You will work collaboratively on high-impact projects and partner with senior leadership to shape data-driven commercial decisions across the organization.

Ready to become part of the most important mission on this planet? We are rebuy, pioneers in the sustainable reuse of electronics and media. and we are looking for you! Our mission: to make circular living accessible to all, one transaction at a time. By giving products a second chance, we reduce resource consumption and avoid unnecessary e-waste.

Our team of 600 comes from all corners of the world; we drive things forward collaboratively, take personal responsibility, value positive critical thinking, and persistently serve our customers and planet.

Key Responsibilities:


Develop and optimize end-to-end pricing strategies, including algorithm development, implementation, and continuous performance monitoring
Design and execute complex pricing experiments and A/B tests, analyzing results and providing strategic recommendations to leadership
Generate commercial insights by conducting deep-dive analyses on pricing elasticity, customer behavior, market dynamics, and competitive positioning
Collaborate with senior stakeholders in Commercial to identify opportunities and solve complex business challenges
Create and automate reporting frameworks and dashboards to enable self-service analytics across the organization
Establish best practices for pricing analytics, experimentation, and commercial decision-making
Stay current with industry trends in pricing optimization, e-commerce analytics, and circular economy business models

Strong proficiency in SQL and any major programming language
Ability to translate complex analyses into clear, actionable business recommendations
Effective stakeholder management and communication skills
Willingness to understand and challenge requirements and contribute your own solutions
Strategic mindset with strong business acumen and commercial awareness
Motivated and organized, thriving in dynamic environments
Willingness to learn and being excited about experimenting with new technology
Experience with pricing algorithms, dynamic pricing, or revenue management systems
Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI) and DBT
Experience in sustainability-focused or circular economy businesses
Understanding of marketplace or two-sided platform dynamics

Berlin
Hybrid model

Work with a friendly team that is passionate about circularity and wants to make the world a little more sustainable, one transaction at a time.

Benefit from flat hierarchies and the greatest possible transparency so that things don't take longer than they have to and you always feel well informed.
Enjoy our centrally located office in Berlin (Erkelenzdamm 11 - 13, 10999 Berlin).
Benefit from our family-friendly, flexible environment and paid child sick leave.
Bring your dog to the office: we love animals and want to cuddle yours, too!
Use employee discounts on rebuy.de, sustainable products at Futurebens, offers for sustainable micro-mobility and massages directly in our office.
Have fun at our legendary team and company events.

Take care of your work-life balance with 30 vacation days.

description_full:
At Moss, we give finance professionals the power to automate their day-to-day and make forward-thinking decisions.

Our team and culture make us unique — we’re driven by impact and growth, where every one of us strives to learn and excel. Recognised by Sifted’s Rising 100 and LinkedIn's Top Startups, we’re here to help propel your career and together, make Moss a lasting success.

We’re looking for a Senior Data Analyst to help our Customer Success team make data-driven decisions that improve our post-sale commercial motion including topics such as customer retention, engagement, and upsells.

Reporting into Moss head of data and insights, you’ll leverage your analytical expertise to build visibility into customer behavior, health, and lifecycle, working closely with CS leadership and other go-to-market teams.

You’ll be part of a growing B2B SaaS business, supporting our mission to help customers realise full value from our platform through actionable data insights.

Your Responsibilities

Here's a bit more on what you can expect to be doing day-to-day:


Deliver customer insights - You will analyse customer usage, adoption, and engagement data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities. Package and present actionable recommendations to senior leaders to drive retention and expansion within our existing customer base.
Build and maintain dashboards - You will designed clear, reliable dashboards to track key CS metrics such as spend, NRR, churn, product adoption, and health scores.
Ensure data accuracy - This includes maintain data quality and reporting integrity, ensuring reliable and consistent metrics for decision-making.
Collaborate cross-functionally - Ongoing partnerships with Customer Success, Revenue Operations, Product, and Finance to align on customer KPIs and data definitions.
Enable proactive actions - You will develop alerts, triggers, or health models that help CSMs easily identify and act on at risk accounts and upsell opportunities.
Support forecasting & strategy - You will align with CS leadership to build retention models, renewal forecasts, and OKR tracking frameworks.
Improving Standards - You will define and improve data processes, reporting standards, and best practices within the CS and data functions.



About You

We think you’ll need the following experience and qualifications to succeed in the role:


Proven experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or revenue operations within a growing B2B SaaS environment.
Exposure to Customer Success teams or similar commercial functions (Sales, RevOps, Product Growth).
SQL working proficiency (non-negotiable) - you’re confident querying and transforming complex datasets.
Experience with Salesforce (preferred) and familiarity with CS tools.
Experience with BI tools (Metabase is a bonus)
Fluent in written and spoken English



In addition, here are the skills and attributes we are looking for:


Technically and Analytically Skilled - You possess the technical knowledge to generate data driven insights and you apply the appropriate analytical methods to get the most out of available data. You adapt your approach to best address the problem at hand.
Impact-led Project Manager - You scope your projects with your team and stakeholders. You aim for high impact and actionable insights. You focus and deliver on projects and communicate deviations from the plan proactively. You have a strong sense of ownership, attention to detail, and a proactive mindset.
Business Expert - You understand the business intricacies and needs and how Moss monetizes its product, and this helps you deeply understand how to collect, process, and interpret the data.
Collaborative Communication - You are able to present your insights and you can adapt to the audience (story tell and explain insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders). You foster open and constructive dialogue within the team.



About Moss

Moss is a SaaS scale-up founded in Berlin, with a team of 300+ people fro

description_full:
Senior Marketing Analyst (m/w/d) - E-Commerce & Online Marketing




EPODEX | Vollzeit | Berichtet direkt an: Head of Marketing / Geschäftsführung




Wer wir sind

EPODEX wächst – und mit uns wächst der Anspruch an datengetriebene Entscheidungen. Wir glauben nicht daran, Bauchgefühl zur Strategie zu machen. Wir glauben daran, dass Daten Geschichten erzählen – wenn man weiß, wie man ihnen zuhört. Genau dafür suchen wir dich.




Deine Mission

Arbeite nicht einfach nur - gestalte gemeinsam mit uns.

Du bist nicht nur Analyst. Du bist der Taktgeber, der datenbasierte Klarheit in unsere Wachstumsstrategie bringt. Du erkennst, was andere übersehen, und verwandelst Zahlen in Entscheidungen, die EPODEX voranbringen. Du arbeitest direkt an der Schnittstelle zwischen Performance, Strategie und Unternehmensführung - sichtbar, wirkungsvoll und nah am Business.

 

Deine Aufgaben

Datenerhebung & Tracking - Die Grundlage für alles

Ohne saubere Daten keine belastbaren Entscheidungen. Du sorgst dafür, dass unser Datenfundament steht - und stabil bleibt.

Du überwachst unser Tracking-Setup ganzheitlich und stellst sicher, dass jede relevante Nutzerinteraktion korrekt und vollständig erfasst wird – über alle Kanäle hinweg.
Du definierst und pflegst eine konsistente Marketing-Parameter-Struktur (z. B. UTM-Taxonomien), die kanalübergreifend Vergleichbarkeit schafft und Auswertungen skalierbar macht.
Du begleitest aktiv die Tag-Manager-Konfiguration und arbeitest eng mit dem Data Engineering zusammen, um neue Tracking-Anforderungen schnell und sauber umzusetzen.
Du etablierst Prozesse zur kontinuierlichen Qualitätssicherung unserer Daten - du erkennst Abweichungen bevor sie zu Fehlentscheidungen führen.

 

Kampagnen-Analyse - Verstehen, was wirklich funktioniert

Du analysierst nicht um der Analyse willen - du analysierst, um zu handeln.

Du bewertest die Performance unserer Kampagnen auf allen relevanten Ebenen: Kanal, Kampagne, Anzeigengruppe, Creatives - und leitest daraus klare Optimierungsimpulse ab.
Du analysierst zentrale KPIs wie ROAS, CPM, CPC und CTR nicht isoliert, sondern im Kontext von Markt, Saison und Wettbewerb - und siehst das Gesamtbild.
Du empfiehlst datenbasiert, wie Budgets zwischen Kanälen und Kampagnen allokiert werden sollten, um maximale Effizienz und Reichweite zu erzielen.
Du erkennst Muster in der Saisonalität und beziehst externe Faktoren (Trends, Marktlage, Plattformveränderungen) aktiv in deine Einschätzungen ein.

 

Conversion & Funnel - Potenziale sehen, Hebel finden

Wachstum entsteht nicht immer durch mehr Traffic - sondern durch besseres Verständnis des Nutzers.

Du analysierst Conversion Rates differenziert nach Kanal, Gerät, Landingpage und Zielgruppe - und weißt, wo wir Nutzer verlieren und warum.
Du identifizierst Funnel-Drop-offs mit chirurgischer Präzision und arbeitest mit dem Team daran, sie systematisch zu schließen.
Du konzipierst und wertest A/B- sowie multivariate Tests sowohl für Paid- als auch für Owned-Channel-Formate aus - von der Hypothese bis zur Handlungsempfehlung.
Du bringst den Testing-Gedanken aktiv ins Unternehmen: Kein Bauchgefühl, kein Zufall - Lernen durch strukturierte Experimente.

 

Attribution - Gerechtigkeit für jeden Touchpoint

Du weißt, dass der letzte Klick selten der entscheidende ist.

Du baust ein belastbares Multi-Touch-Attributionsmodell auf, das abbildet, wie unsere Kanäle zusammenspielen - und wo der echte Wertbeitrag liegt.
Du definierst, pflegst und vergleichst verschiedene Attributionsmodelle und zeigst transparent auf, welche Entscheidungen sie beeinflussen.
Du erkennst Kanal-Kannibalisierung frühzeitig und entwickelst Maßnahmen, um Ressourcen zielgerichtet und ohne Reibungsverlust einzusetzen.
Du schaffst die Grundlage dafür, dass Budget-Entscheidungen nicht mehr auf Annahmen, sondern auf echtem Verständnis der Customer Journey basieren.




Was du mitbringst

Fachliche Qualifikationen

Mindestens 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in der datengetriebenen Analyse vo

description_full:
Role Overview:

AVATR is looking for a Product, Market & User Research Manager (Europe) to join our growing team in Munich. This role will lead the European product, market and user research team, be fully responsible for formulating and implementing the overall research strategy for the European region, oversee in-depth research on overseas (focusing on European) market product status, collect and analyse dealer and user feedback, track regional competitor dynamics and regulatory changes, output cost-effective overseas product configuration lists and professional research reports, and . The role will also be responsible for team management, capacity building and cross-departmental resource coordination to ensure the efficient operation of the research team and the effective landing of research results.



What You Will Do:


Team Leadership & Management: Lead the European product, market and user research team, formulate teamwork plans and performance indicators, be responsible for the recruitment, training, assessment and development of team members, build a professional research team, and improve the overall professional ability and work efficiency of the team.
Overall Research Strategy Formulation & Implementation: Develop the overall product, market and user research strategy for the European region and phased implementation plans, clarify research priorities and directions, integrate research resources, and ensure that the research work is aligned with the company's overall development strategy and business needs.
Market & Product Research Oversight: Oversee in-depth research on overseas (focusing on European) market product status, collect and sort out feedback from dealers and end-users, track regional competitor dynamics, product configuration plans and core selling points, establish a systematic research database, and provide comprehensive market and product insights.
Product Configuration & Regulatory Research: Lead the team to analyse regional preferences for specific product configurations (value perception) and relevant regulatory requirements on configurations in different European regions, output overseas product configuration lists that maximize value while controlling costs, and provide support for product optimization and compliance.
Competitor & Market Segment Research: Focus on the research of European regional markets, oversee the monitoring and analysis of competitor business dynamics, market product competition situation, tariff policies, local policies and regulatory changes, research and analyse the scale of segmented markets and competitor sales performance, and provide data support for market positioning and business decision-making.
Research Report Output & Strategy Support: Oversee the sorting, analysis and induction of research data, guide the team to output high-quality regular and special research reports (including product positioning analysis, competitor selection analysis, pricing analysis, EAP, EATP, etc.) and provide professional suggestions and strategic support for cross-departmental decision-making.
Cross-departmental Collaboration & Resource Coordination: Act as the core contact for European product, market and user research work, cooperate closely with product, marketing, design, sales, legal and other cross-functional teams, coordinate resources, promote the effective application of research results in product development, market promotion and other links, and solve cross-departmental research-related needs.
Research System Optimization: Continuously optimize the European product, market and user research methods, processes and tools, promote the innovation of research models, improve the accuracy, efficiency and pertinence of research work, and adapt to the dynamic changes of the European market.




What You Will Bring:


Bachelor's degree or above in Marketing, Market Research, Product Management, Business Administration or related majors; master's degree is preferred.
5-8 years

Lion Studios is a mobile games publisher that works with mobile developers to get their games discovered by players around the world. Headquartered in San Francisco, Lion Studios’ creative and strategy teams utilize their expertise to help game developers break through the noise and grow their businesses.

Since launching in 2018, Lion Studios has driven 90% of its games to the Top 10 in the App Store and Google Play and helped drive 16 games to #1 in the US App Store’s Top Free chart.

Role Overview

As a Junior Growth Analyst at Lion Studios, you’ll play a key role in driving the success of our mobile games through data-driven marketing and user acquisition. This is an entry-level position ideal for recent graduates or early-career professionals who are passionate about mobile gaming and eager to learn performance marketing. You’ll start by managing user acquisition campaigns on paid platforms, gaining hands-on experience with campaign setup, optimization, and performance analysis.

Key Responsibilities


Managing the full UA process for our mobile game portfolio - Planning and execution of marketing campaigns.
Overseeing the marketing campaigns across multiple channels and platforms.
Producing comprehensive reporting, analysis of ROI and identifying new opportunities to support our key games.
Experimenting and analysis of campaigns for existing and new games.
Developing briefings for ad creatives and helping with the production of marketing materials.
Work cross-functionally with other teams to improve game performance and monetization.
Managing business relationships and day-to-day communications with external partners and networks.
Leveraging tools and automation (including AI-based solutions) to improve campaign efficiency and workflows.


Required Skills, Knowledge And Expertise


Analytical and metric-driven.
Fast learner with the ability to be adaptable.
Organised with high attention to detail.
Ability to work in a collaborative, fast paced environment.
Available and eligible to work in Berlin.
Interest in mobile games (you play them, follow trends, or have researched the space).

Educational background in an analytical field

Familiarity with mobile marketing KPIs (CPI, ROAS, LTV, retention, etc.)
Exposure to platforms like AppLovin, Meta Ads, or Google Ads
Experience with Excel/Google Sheets or basic data analysis tools

As a part of the Brand Media & Measurement team, you will manage digital campaign planning, A/B testing, and reporting across key digital channels like Meta, Twitter, YouTube etc., and also be responsible for management & analysis of brand research projects.

This person will have the opportunity to learn and grow with the global Brand Marketing team based in Thailand. They will play a key role in analyzing all kind of campaign & research data to deliver insights that will shape the team’s strategy and fulfil our ambition of creating an engaging and meaningful brand in the tech-and-travel space.

This role is based out of Agoda’s Global Operations Center in Bangkok, Thailand, giving the successful applicant the once-in-a-lifetime chance to work in one of the world’s most exciting and fast-paced cities, close to some of the most iconic travel destinations, with a fun and talented team. We provide complete relocation for you and your family and excellent benefits.

We ensure the brand is always looking and sounding its best in everything we do, whether on the world stage or in a company newsletter.

Learn: Communicate test results & campaign learnings to the team and broader stakeholders through comprehensive analysis, reporting and data visualization. Develop key learnings for different media platforms and markets. 
Analyse: Delve into data from digital tests & research studies to gather useful insights for optimization of brand campaigns. 
Collaborate: Liaise with external media partners (e.g. Google, Meta) for planning, analysis and execution, and consolidate media data and analysis with digital activity being run in-house.


What you’ll need to succeed:


A minimum of a bachelor’s degree in mathematics, marketing, communication, business, or another related fie

When travelers are searching for a hotel, we want the obvious choice to be trivago! Our leading metasearch engine is super fast and constantly optimized - enabling millions of travelers to compare hotel prices from hundreds of booking sites and find great deals in just a few clicks. We use cutting-edge technology, real-time auction, and machine learning techniques with petabytes of data to create an experience - time and money saved! In the lively city of Düsseldorf, we seize opportunities to learn everyday, innovate, and make an enduring mark on the travel industry. At trivago you will find those who aren’t afraid of change but rather embrace it, turning every challenge into a pathway for growth. Join trivago, work with a great team, and grow with us!



Join us in making a difference



The Marketing Strategy and Intelligence team at trivago is a unique blend of data and business expertise, steering key marketing decisions through impactful data products, actionable insights, and deep market understanding. As a Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in shaping and executing trivago's global marketing strategy. Your work will empower teams to make informed choices that drive growth, efficiency, and innovation across all marketing channels.

You will collaborate closely with data scientists, analysts, engineers as well as growth managers and business stakeholders to transform complex data into real business value. Together, you will design advanced analytics solutions, develop insightful dashboards and reports, and uncover strategic opportunities through in-depth research, modeling and experimentation. By providing clear, actionable insights, you will help optimize campaigns, identify market trends, and guide the decision-making process.

If this sounds like you, read on & apply today!



How you’ll make an impact:


Translate data into actionable insights that shape our marketing strategy and investments.
Partner with marketing stakeholders to identify opportunities, define KPIs, and measure success.
Build clear, engaging dashboards and reports that highlight trends and tell compelling stories.
Conduct deep dives to understand performance drivers, uncover anomalies, and recommend actions.
Ensure marketing data quality and consistency across multiple sources.
Share your insights in a way that resonates with both technical and non-technical audiences.


What you'll need to thrive:


5+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, ideally in marketing, e-commerce, or digital growth.
A degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, or another quantitative field.
Strong business acumen - you connect the dots between numbers, customer behavior, and company goals.
Proficiency in SQL and hands-on experience with BI/dashboarding tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker).
Proven ability to tell stories with data - making complex findings clear, engaging, and actionable.
Solid understanding of marketing metrics, attribution, and campaign performance.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.


Stand out with:


Experience in the travel, e-commerce, or digital marketing industry.
Hands-on experience with A/B testing, geo testing, or causal inference.
Knowledge of big data platforms like BigQuery.
Familiarity with Python or R for analysis.

Worried about missing a few requirements? Still apply, and express your motivation as you may just be the right candidate for this or other roles!



What you can look forward to:



As you grow, so do we.

This is why at trivago, we prioritize your development, offer personalized coaching through Nilo, and provide workshops, educational meetups, conferences, free online learning courses, and access to a fully-equipped campus library.

Moving to join us?

No problem. You can count on the visa support from our talent support team, a relocation package, interest-free newcomer loan, free language classes, regular team and company-wide events to build experiences together.

description_full:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage within BCG's Operations and Marketing, Sales and Pricing Practice Areas you will work in a growing global team, providing industry/functional expertise and insights whilst working together with case and proposal teams to provide knowledge assets, analysis and expert advisory. You’ll serve as a thought partner and content expert to case teams and topic/sector leaders, helping structure and solve complex issues.

You will support commercialization efforts for the Customer Services topic working, in conjunction with business leaders, through contributing analysis and insights to proposals, client workshops and marketing materials. Additionally, as a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, you will contribute to developing intellectual property & assets for the business and assist in onboarding and training junior colleagues based on your topic/sector expertise.

The Customer Services and Contact Centers Topic Area is an expert network of knowledge team members and experienced consultants who help clients on specific cases related to typical contact center and customer service improvement levers (e.g., waste contact reduction, handling time optimization, digital migration, productivity improvement, queuing logics, IVR optimization, etc.).

You will have substantive experience using technology and process innovation in Customer Service and Contact Centers. In addition, expertise in developing a strategy, managing demand, deploying capacity, enable talent, and adopting new ways of working.

YOU'RE GOOD AT


Solving client problems through formulating relevant research and/or analytical approaches in your area of expertise, namely Customer Service and Contact Centers
Conducting call center data analysis to assess drivers of demand and performance
Identifying digital service and AI optimization levers to improve operational efficiency
Defining contact center improvement business cases and delivery plans
Communicating with case teams and stakeholders, in a credible and confident way
Working collaboratively and effectively in a group dynamic often virtual, proficient in agile ways of working
Being flexible and bringing a curious and creative mindset, open to new things and able to propose innovative ideas
Navigating complexity and ambiguity


What You'll Bring


2+ years of consulting experience in relevant with relevance to Customer Service and Contact Centers; candidates with consulting experience preferred
In lieu of consulting experience, 3+ years minimum industry experience required; 4-6+ years of industry experience strongly preferred. Ideally experience in big Contact Center (>5,000 FTEs) transformations/operations with a major service provider / brand (e.g. retail bank, telecoms provider, utility, airline,…), Or, relevant tech firm (Zendesk, Nice, Qualtrics) doing product management and / or product marketing.
Experience with a combination of technology (RPA, AI), operations and people challenges
Bachelor's Degree required (advanced degree preferred)
Fluency in English; German language preferr

Hello👋I am Willian, Trading and Analytics Lead at Kayzen, and I am now looking for a Performance Campaign Analyst to join our team 🙌

About Kayzen

Kayzen is a mobile demand-side platform (DSP) dedicated to democratizing programmatic advertising. We enable leading apps, agencies, media buyers, and brands to run programmatic customer acquisition, retargeting, and brand performance campaigns through our self-serve and managed service options. Built on the three core pillars of performance, transparency, and control, Kayzen powers the world's best mobile marketing teams with bespoke solutions that fuel business growth and deliver a competitive advantage. With an unprecedented scale of 300B+ daily ad requests from 1.6B+ unique users worldwide, we serve up to 1B+ ads per day in 180 countries. Kayzen is accessible through our APIs and user interface.

The Team

The Trading team is responsible for creating growth through the management and optimization of mobile performance advertising campaigns for clients utilizing the managed service offering. The goal of the team is to deliver on the client's objectives through established or new trading best practices and communicate account status regularly and effectively with their CSM counterparts.

Why this role

This is a hands-on trading role where you'll manage mobile programmatic campaigns across verticals like gaming, fintech, and betting, optimizing toward CPA/ROAS targets while learning to scale efficiently. You'll work closely with senior traders and cross-functional partners, gaining exposure to advanced analytics, LLM-powered workflows, and trading strategies that go well beyond what most desks offer at this level. If you're analytical, curious about automation, and want to accelerate your programmatic career, this is the role for you.

What you'll do

Trading excellence


Manage day-to-day campaign optimization across a set of advertisers; monitor and hit KPI targets (CPA, ROAS, retention) with guidance from senior traders.
Execute supply-side and audience optimization tactics including publisher management, bid adjustments, and dayparting across ad formats (banner, video, native, CTV).
Run A/B tests on bids, budgets, creatives, and geos as part of broader testing roadmaps; document results and contribute learnings to the Trading Playbook.
Monitor pacing and budget allocation daily, flagging risks and under-delivery proactively.
Build a working understanding of mobile attribution (postback flows, attribution windows, MMP integrations) and how attribution models affect how we measure and optimize campaign performance.


Analytics & automation


Use SQL to pull and analyze campaign data from internal databases; build comfort with joins, CTEs, and window functions over time.
Learn to leverage LLMs (GPT/Claude) for recurring analyses, data briefs, and performance diagnostics, this is a core part of how our team works.
Support the creation and maintenance of dashboards and reporting tools used by the trading team.
Develop your analytical instincts: learn to ask the right questions, understand what's driving performance, and use available tools (SQL, AI, BI tools) to find answers.


Cross-functional impact


Work with CSMs to understand client goals and communicate campaign status clearly.
Support QBR preparation and client-facing deliverables alongside senior traders.
Collaborate with Product Support to troubleshoot delivery issues and with Supply on inventory quality questions.


Since I am based in Argentina and other key product and customer success team members are located mostly across EMEA and India, we are open to a remote setup of this position. For optimal overlap with customers' time zones and internal teams your main working location should preferably be close to the CET time zone.

Requirements


1–3 years of programmatic ad buying, ad operations, or adjacent adtech experience.
Familiarity with MMPs (AppsFlyer, Adjus

Job Description

About the role:

This is a unique opportunity to shape how we use analytics to improve and grow our app experience. In this role, you'll drive performance insights that truly impact product and marketing strategies—right at the intersection of data and decision-making. As a Digital Analytics Manager – App (m/f/d), you are responsible for the digital analytics setup and performance measurement of our app.

In this role, you also work on web analytics topics, ensuring consistent tracking, reporting, and KPIs across platforms. You focus on analytics foundations, data quality, and insights to support product and marketing decision-making.

About Your Tasks


Own and develop the analytics setup for our app from the ground up
Design and manage tracking concepts, KPIs, and performance dashboards
Analyze user journeys, funnels and generate actionable insights
Ensure high data quality, compliance and documentation standards
Collaborate closely with product, marketing, and tech to roll out new tracking implementations


Qualifications

About you:


You bring solid experience in mobile analytics and know your way around attribution tools like AppsFlyer or Adjust
Experience in digital analytics with a strong focus on app analytics; web analytics experience is a plus 
You understand tracking frameworks and what makes analytics truly valuable
You enjoy working cross-functionally and want to shape how decisions are made through data
You’re structured, curious, and value a collaborative team culture


Additional Information

Benefits

In order to provide you with the best possible support for your individual needs - whether it‘s living a healthy life, having enough time for your family, or developing your skills - we offer a wide range of different, site-specific benefits:


Welcome days: We want you to feel at home with us from the very first day. Therefore, we welcome you with our comprehensive onboarding program.
Sports: Stay healthy. Profit from a membership (M) package at Urban Sports Club, so that you can take advantage of a huge variety of sport offers.
Work from Home: If your job does not require you to be present in the office, we can arrange the place you work from individually - even for up to 20 days a year anywhere in the EU.
Corporate pension plan: We offer a company pension scheme and have a dedicated external consultant to offer you the best guidance and advice.

Tätigkeiten


Du verantwortest fünfstellige Tagesbudgets auf unseren Paid Channels (Meta, Instagram, Google, YouTube & Co).
Du erstellst unsere Kampagnen, findest skalierbare Kampagnen-Setups und optimierst diese stetig im Hinblick auf unsere Kampagnenziele.
Du analysierst & optimierst die Customer Journey von über 1 Mio. jährlichen Nutzern auf unserer Seite.
Du testest täglich neue Werbeanzeigen, tauschst dich mit unserem Creative-Team über die Ad-Performance aus und gibst Impulse zur Verbesserung.
Du stehst in direktem Kontakt zu unseren Facebook und Google Account Manager:innen, um wichtige operative Schritte zu besprechen.


Team

Du arbeitest in einem Team aus 5 Performance Marketern zusammen und hast enge Überschneidungen mit dem Creative Team.

Über das Unternehmen

Hi! 🙋 Wir sind die Uniwunder GmbH, ein junges Tech- und Marketing Unternehmen. Wir haben es uns zur Aufgabe gemacht, Studierende & Absolvent*innen über das universitäre Angebot hinaus zu ihrem vollen Erfolgspotenzial zu führen.

Was das heißt?

Für Studis: Den Erhalt eines digitalen Anlaufpunktes zu den Themen Karriere, Finanzen, Ausland, Studienorientierung und Lernen. Sie finden bei uns das, „was sie in der Uni nicht lernen“.

Und für uns: Ein Arbeitsplatz, bei dem wir mit Innovativität und Kreativität gemeinsam über uns selbst hinauswachsen und schneller & smarter skalieren als unsere Mitbewerber. Wir finden digitale Lösungen für reale Probleme im Studenten- und Absolventenbereich und sind stolz darauf, jährlich >200.000 Studis & Absolvent*innen zu bereichern.

Da wir stets und ständig wachsen und unsere Geschäftsbereiche weiter ausbauen wollen, suchen wir kluge Köpfe, die Bock haben mit uns Großes zu erreichen.

Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!

Klicke auf "Bewerben" für Details zu den Benefits, dem Team und dem Bewerbungsprozess.





Desired Skills and Experience

Online Marketing, Google Analytics, Performance Marketing, Facebook Kampagnen, Analytics, User Journeys

Wer wir sind

Die inhabergeführte Krieger Unternehmensgruppe setzt mit ihren über 10.000 Mitarbeitern seit sehr als 50 Jahren neue Maßstäbe im Handel- und Immobilienbereich. Sie vereint starke und namhafte Möbelhausmarken wie Höffner, Möbel Kraft, Sconto, Möbel Mahler, aber auch E-Commerce Handelsmarken wie z. B. Design24 oder sofa.de unter sich. 

Die KOS GmbH & Co. KG – als Teil der Krieger Digital – betreut den E-Commerce-Auftritt unserer Möbelhausmarken. Unser Digital Marketing Management verantwortet nicht nur alle relevanten digitalen Marketingkanäle der Krieger Gruppe sondern auch ihre digitalen Performance Kanäle, um unsere Kunden auf allen relevanten Plattformen effizient und effektiv zu erreichen. Deine Aufgaben

Als Senior Performance Marketing Manager (w/m/d) bist Du verantwortlich für den Ausbau der Feed-basierten Produktdaten-Werbung auf Google und Bing und weiteren digitalen Kanälen in der Krieger-Gruppe. Dein direkter Schwerpunkt sind dabei die Product-Listing-Ads auf Google und Bing, sowie weiteren Portalen, wie zum Beispiel idealo. Dabei arbeitest Du eng mit Kampagnen-, Segmentierungs- und Performance-Marketing-Spezialisten sowie unserem Data Team zusammen. Du berichtest direkt an den Leiter Digitales Marketing.

Deine Aufgaben: 

Du bist verantwortlich für den Ausbau der Product-Listing-Ads auf Google und Bing und weiteren Feed-basierten Plattformen
Du optimierst die Produktdarstellung in unseren Marketingkanälen mittel Feed-Management-Systemen wie Feed Dynamix 
Du steuerst KUR-basiert das Wachstum
Du erarbeitest gemeinsam mit Deinen Kollegen kanalübergreifende Kampagnen, um die User Journey gezielt zu attributisieren und optimale Ziele zu erreichen
Du optimierst das Channel-Reporting, welches Du gemeinsam mit dem Data Team initiierst und kontinuierlich im Blick behältst

Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium, von Vorteil sind Betriebswirtschaft (Schwerpunkt Marketing) oder Wirtschaftsinformatik mit Schwerpunkt Marketing

Du hast bereits 2 bis 3 Jahre Erfahrungen im Performance Marketing gesammelt und kennst Dich mit Umsatzzielen, ROAS/KuR-Vorgaben und PPC-Gebotsstrategien aus
Du bringst exzellente analytische Fähigkeiten mit, kannst sicher auftreten, Menschen überzeugen und motivieren
Du gehst voran, kommunizierst sicher und behältst als Teamplayer in stressigen Situationen einen kühlen Kopf
